name: Erich Wolfgang Korngold
abstract: Erich Wolfgang Korngold (German: [ˈeːʁɪç ˈvɔlfɡaŋ ˈkɔʁnɡɔlt]; May 29, 1897 – November 29, 1957) was an Austrian composer and conductor, who left Europe in the mid-1930s and later adopted US nationality. A child prodigy, he became one of the most important and influential composers in Hollywood history.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: I look to people like Nelson Riddle, Axle Stordahl and even people with film scores like Erich Korngold, you just listen to what’s going on behind the action.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012
